name: Rice & Peas with Tempeh
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsweetened coconut, ground or shredded
1/2 Red or green bell pepper, diced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, heat 2 1/2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat. Add the brown rice and coconut; sauté for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring constantly.
2. Pour in the water and cinnamon stick. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until all the liquid has evaporated (about 40 minutes).
3. In a separate pot or large skillet, cook black-eyed peas with bay leaves (1 teaspoon dried) in salted water for about 20 minutes, or until tender. Drain and discard the bay leaves.
4. In another pan, heat vegetable oil over medium heat. Add onion and garlic; sauté until onions soften.
5. Stir in chili and bell pepper; cook for a couple of minutes.
6. Add tempeh, fennel seeds, salt, and black pepper to the skillet. Lower heat and cover. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the tempeh is golden brown and slightly crisp (about 10 minutes).
7. Combine cooked rice and coconut with the peas, seasoning mixture, and scallions.
8. Serve hot, or reheat in a moderate oven for 10 minutes before serving. Top with additional scallions.
